arts.ttl: Sheffield Wednesday boss Danny Röhl makes key pledge ahead of Hull City clash
arts.sum: That result, coming on the back of a 1-0 loss at bitter rivals Sheffield United before the international break, leaves the Owls 15th in the table, four points above 22nd place City, who have not won in eight and were beaten 1-0 at Kenilworth Road on Saturday, their third loss on the spin. I am happy with the performance of my players and the things we did but of course we need to win these games," the Owls coach said .

arts.ttl: Hull City man assesses Sheffield Wednesday challenge amid team's MKM Stadium troubles
arts.sum: And beating Wednesday in front of their own supporters would be a great way to end what has been a disappointing couple of months and end what has since become an eight-game winless run, with three defeats on the spin and a growing sense of gloom above the MKM Stadium, and that was before Storm Bert took hold this weekend. Ivor Pandur admits he's under no illusions about the importance of earning local bragging rights when Hull City take on old foes Sheffield Wednesday tonight.

arts.ttl: Hull City's squad need confidence, not criticism, after Championship form slump
arts.sum: In a recent home game, skipper Lewie Coyle raced from right to left, in front of Walter and in trying to play a ball out to the left flank, ending up getting his compass slightly off and out of play it went. There are no shortage of reasons why the Tigers find themselves 22nd in the Championship with just 15 points from their opening bout of games: a change in playing style under the new manager, a frenetic late recruitment drive and a lack of clinical striker coupled with a host of injuries to potential match-winning players.

arts.ttl: 'The world is against us' - Tim Walter's Hull City admission ahead of Sheffield Wednesday clash - Hull Live
arts.sum: That defeat at Kenilworth Road saw Walter jeered again by the away fans, claiming he didn't go to applaud their backing because he was wet, but in truth, it was a game his side should never have lost with Joao Pedro passing up a glorious chance in each half to turn the game in City's favour, and that only added to his annoyance. Tim Walter says it's up to him and his players to battle out of their worrying predicament which sees Hull City mired in the Championship's relegation zone and with the German boss believing the world is against his team at the moment.
